title: 10 best herbal essential oils for oily skin
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for oily skin are a natural remedy that helps to balance and regulate the skin's oil production, reducing the appearance of acne and other skin issues. The best essential oils for oily skin include Lavandula angustifolia, Melaleuca alternifolia, Thymus vulgaris, Rosmarinus officinalis, Cymbopogon citratus, Eucalyptus globulus, Pelargonium graveolens, Geranium maculatum, Cymbopogon nardus, and Salvia officinalis. If left untreated, oily skin can lead to low self-esteem, social anxiety, and emotional distress, negatively impacting daily life and relationships.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antibacterial properties. It helps to control sebum production, reducing the appearance of pores and leaving skin looking clearer and more balanced. Additionally, tea tree oil has antifungal properties that combat acne-causing bacteria, allowing for a reduction in breakouts and inflammation. By incorporating tea tree essential oils into your skincare routine, you can say goodbye to greasy complexions and hello to a smoother, healthier-looking complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Tea Tree Essential Oil's antibacterial properties help to combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing inflammation and preventing future breakouts on oily skin.
Antifungal: The antifungal properties of Tea Tree Essential Oil help to control fungal overgrowth on the skin, which can contribute to oily skin issues such as blackheads and whiteheads.
Astringent: Tea Tree Essential Oil's astringent properties help to balance and tighten the pores, reducing oil production and excess sebum buildup that can lead to oily skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain a high concentration of cineole, a compound known for its antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making them ideal for regulating oil production in the skin.
Buds: Tea tree buds have been shown to possess potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and irritation associated with oily skin conditions like acne.
Roots: The roots of the tea tree plant are rich in antioxidants, which can help neutralize free radicals and soothe inflammation in the skin, making them beneficial for calming oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves in the early morning for optimal oil quality and potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, dry, dark place for at least 7 days to prevent spoilage and oxidation.
Steam-distill the dried leaves using 10% w/w water and 1:5 leaf-to-water ratio for 6-8 hours to extract essential oil.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sedimentation.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark glass containers, labeling with date, batch number, and recommended usage guidelines of 3-5% dilution for skin care products.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with oily skin because it has natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help to balance the skin's natural pH levels, reducing excess oil production. It also contains antioxidants that help to combat environmental stressors and inflammation, which can contribute to clogged pores and acne. By using English lavender essential oils on oily skin, you can experience a reduction in shine and an improvement in overall complexion, leaving your skin looking healthy and radiant.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: English Lavender oil's antimicrobial properties help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing breakouts caused by bacterial or fungal infections.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants in English Lavender oil neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to the skin cells, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with oily skin, while also helping to regulate sebum production.

Regulatory property: English Lavender oil's regulatory properties help to balance the skin's natural pH and regulate sebum production, reducing the appearance of pores and controlling excess oil production.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and regulate sebum production, making them ideal for balancing oily skin.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain a high concentration of camphor, a natural antiseptic that helps to prevent breakouts and calm acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in sufficient quantity for distillation.
Wash the harvested flowers thoroughly with distilled water to remove impurities and debris.
Steam-distill the washed flowers using a copper alembic still with 10:1 flower-to-water ratio at 100°c temperature.
Collect the resulting essential oil after 30 minutes of distillation and filter it through paper or cloth to remove particles.
Store the extracted lavandula angustifolia essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids to preserve its quality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. It has the ability to balance the skin's natural oil production, reducing excess sebum that can clog pores and cause acne. Additionally, lemongrass essential oil contains citral, a compound that has been shown to regulate skin's natural moisture levels, leaving skin looking clearer and more balanced. When used in skincare products or applied directly to the skin, lemongrass essential oil helps to purify and refine the complexion, making it an effective remedy for oily skin types.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Lemongrass oil has antimicrobial properties that help combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing inflammation and preventing future breakouts, making it an effective treatment for oily skin.
Astringent property: The astringent properties of lemongrass oil help to tighten pores and reduce excess oil production, leaving the skin feeling refreshed and balanced.
Inflammation-reducing property: Lemongrass oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with oily skin.
remedy_parts: Stems: The stems of lemongrass contain high amounts of citral, a natural astringent that helps to balance the production of sebum in the skin.
Roots: Lemongrass roots have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including acne and oily skin, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of cymbopogon citratus from the garden or storehouse weighing around 50 grams.
Dry the harvested leaves in shade for 24 hours using a clean mesh screen allowing air circulation.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a distillation apparatus with 200 ml of water at 100°c for 60 minutes.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled mixture by decantation and store it in a dark glass bottle within 1 week.
Filter the essential oil again using a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities before final storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with oily skin because of its unique properties that work to balance and regulate sebum production. The oil's natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce the appearance of pores, while its astringent properties help to tighten and refine the skin, reducing excess oiliness. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum essential oils' ability to calm and soothe the skin also helps to reduce redness and irritation associated with oily skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eucalyptus Globulus Essential Oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne-prone or oily skin.
Antibacterial: The oil's antibacterial properties can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, which contribute to acne and clogged pores in oily skin.
Decongestant: Eucalyptus Globulus Essential Oil has natural decongestant properties that can help reduce the appearance of pores and tighten the skin, giving it a more balanced appearance.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of antimicrobial compounds that help reduce acne-causing bacteria and inflammation, making them effective in treating oily skin.
Buds: The buds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning when the dew has evaporated ensuring optimal aromatic content.
Dry the harvested leaves in small bunches using a clean and dry cloth to prevent contamination within 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves using 10% water concentration for 4 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cotton cloth removing any impurities in 30 minutes.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight lids storing it in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antibacterial properties. It effectively reduces sebum production, which is often the root cause of oily skin. The oil's antiseptic properties also help to combat acne-causing bacteria, preventing breakouts and blackheads. Additionally, thyme essential oil has a clarifying effect on the skin, allowing it to breathe and function more efficiently, reducing the appearance of pores and leaving the complexion looking balanced and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme essential oil has been shown to effectively combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing the risk of infection and inflammation associated with oily skin.
Astringent properties: The astringent properties of thyme essential oil help to constrict pores and reduce excess sebum production, leaving the skin looking cleaner and more balanced.

Antifungal properties: Thyme's antifungal properties can help to control the growth of fungus that can contribute to oily skin conditions such as acne and eczema, promoting a healthier skin environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, thyme roots help to reduce inflammation and soothe acne-prone skin.
Leaves: Thyme leaves contain rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that helps to regulate sebum production and balance the skin's natural pH.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves at peak potency for optimal oil extraction.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove impurities and debris.
Steam-distill the washed thymus vulgaris leaves in a double boiler setup for 30 minutes.
Cool the distillation apparatus slowly by wrapping it in a towel for 20 minutes before separating essential oils.
Filter the resulting oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. It effectively reduces sebum production, balancing the skin's natural oil levels and tightening pores, leaving the skin looking refined and matte. Additionally, rosemary oil's antibacterial properties help to combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing inflammation and redness, making it an effective treatment for oily skin prone to breakouts.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Rosemary essential oil has antibacterial properties that help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to acne and oily skin conditions.
Antifungal: Rosemary essential oil's antifungal properties help to combat fungal infections that can cause skin problems like dandruff, itchiness, and oily skin.
Astringent: Rosemary essential oil has astringent properties that help to tighten the pores and reduce excess oil production, making it an effective natural remedy for balancing oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation associated with oily skin.
Leaves: Rosemary leaves are high in camphor, a natural antiseptic that helps to control acne-causing bacteria and reduce redness in oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and stems for the highest concentration of camphor and borneol compounds.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for at least 24 hours.
Place dried rosmarinus officinalis in a clean glass jar and cover it with high-proof vodka or ethanol for a ratio of 1 part herb to 4 parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction of essential oils from the plant material.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids after allowing the oil to settle for at least 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ironwort essential oils helps with oily skin because it has natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help to balance the skin's pH levels, reducing excess oil production. It also contains antioxidants that combat free radicals, which can cause inflammation and clogged pores. When used in skincare routines, ironwort essential oils can help to tighten pores, absorb excess oil, and leave skin feeling refreshed and matte. As a result, it is an effective natural remedy for managing oily skin and achieving a healthy, balanced complexion.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Ironwort has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial growth that contributes to oily skin and acne.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The plant's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with oily skin and acne.
Soothing and calming properties: Ironwort has a calming effect on the skin, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin caused by excessive oil production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antimicrobial compounds that help to regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation associated with oily skin.
Barks: Containing astringent properties that can help to tighten pores and reduce oiliness, making it an effective ingredient for acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh sideritis scardica leaves at dawn, carefully avoiding any contaminated or damaged leaves.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days, turning them daily to ensure even drying.
Combine 200ml of carrier oil with 20g of dried sideritis scardica leaves in a clean glass jar, covering it tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, allowing the herbs to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and preserving the clear essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with oily skin because of its unique properties that help to regulate sebum production, balance oil levels, and purify the pores. The oil's antimicrobial and antifungal qualities also prevent bacterial and fungal growth, reducing acne and inflammation. Additionally, curry plant oil has natural astringent and toning effects that help to tighten the skin and reduce the appearance of pores, leaving the skin looking smoother and more radiant.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin, while also promoting healthy skin regeneration.
Astringent Properties: Curry Plant oil's astringent properties help to tighten pores, reduce sebum production, and balance the skin's natural pH, leaving it looking clearer and more even-toned.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in regulating oil production and soothing acne-prone skin.
Leaves: Containing potent volatile oils that help to balance sebum production, reduce inflammation, and combat bacterial growth on the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helichrysum italicum leaves and flowers for distillation.
Set up the distillation apparatus using a still or alembic.
Begin the steam distillation process by adding distilled water.
Collect the essential oil as it separates from the distillate.
Store the helichrysum italicum essential oil in a dark place at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood essential oils helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's ability to reduce sebum production and tighten pores makes it an effective remedy for controlling excess oil and unclogging pores, leaving the skin looking clearer and more balanced. Additionally, wormwood's antimicrobial properties help combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin. Astringent: Wormwood essential oil's astringent properties help to constrict pores, reducing their size and minimizing the appearance of pores, making it effective in controlling sebum production and reducing the look of oily skin.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wormwood contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in controlling oil production and soothing oily skin.
Buds: Wormwood buds are rich in camphor, a compound that helps to reduce sebum production and has antifungal properties, making it ideal for treating acne-prone and oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers in the early morning dew.
Dry the plant material by spreading it thinly on paper towels for 7-10 days.
Steam-distill the dried plant material using a copper still at atmospheric pressure for 4 hours.
Monitor the distillation process to collect the essential oil as it separates from the hydrosol at 230-240°f.
Transfer the collected essential oil to dark glass bottles and store in a cool, dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. It helps to balance the skin's natural oil production, reducing excess sebum and purifying the pores. The oil also has anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with acne-prone skin. Additionally, fennel essential oils have antimicrobial properties that prevent bacteria from multiplying, further reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy, balanced complexion.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Fennel oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing inflammation and preventing breakouts on oily skin.

Astringent and antiseptic properties: Fennel oil's astringent and antiseptic properties can help tighten pores and reduce sebum production, minimizing the appearance of pores and controlling shine on oily skin.

remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which help to balance and regulate sebum production in the skin.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems from the garden in the morning after dew has dried off.
Wash the harvested plant material with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the washed plant material using a food dehydrator set at 60 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material in a clevenger apparatus using 5 kg of foeniculum vulgare per liter of water for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper into a dark glass bottle and store in a cool place.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily skin with herbal essential oils is to focus on balancing and regulating oil production. Use a blend of tea tree oil, known for its antibacterial properties, and lavender oil, which helps to calm and soothe the skin. Mix these oils with a carrier oil like jojoba or grapeseed oil and apply it to the face to reduce shine and minimize pores. Regular use can lead to a reduction in excess oil production and a more balanced complexion.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily skin that are treated with herbal essential oils are acne, rosacea, and eczema. Geranium oil is often used to balance sebum production and reduce inflammation, while lavender oil can help soothe and calm the skin. Tea tree oil has antiseptic properties, making it effective against acne-causing bacteria, while chamomile oil can help reduce redness and irritation associated with rosacea and eczema.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with oily skin because it possesses natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help balance the skin's pH levels and reduce sebum production. Additionally, the calming and soothing effects of lavender oil can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to oily skin. remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps to reduce sebum production and inflammation in the skin, thereby controlling oily skin.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to balance the skin's natural pH and reduce oiliness.
Caryophyllene: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which can help to soothe and calm oily skin, reducing its appearance and associated symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their ability to help balance and regulate the skin's natural oil production.
Flowers: Utilized for their antiseptic, antifungal, and antibacterial properties that help to reduce acne and prevent infection.
Stems: Employed for their astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to tighten pores and prevent oil buildup.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh flowers at peak bloom in the early morning to ensure highest oil content.
Steam distill the harvested flowers at 100°c using a steam distillation apparatus for 2 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separating funnel.
Transfer the separated essential oil to a dark glass bottle and seal it tightly.
Label and date the bottle and store it in a secure location out of reach of children.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with oily skin because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. It has the ability to regulate sebum production, reducing the appearance of pores and minimizing oiliness. The oil's astringent properties also help to tighten the skin, controlling excess oil and preventing clogged pores. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help to combat acne-causing bacteria, promoting a clear and balanced complexion. This makes Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil a popular choice for those looking to manage oily skin and achieve a healthier, more radiant complexion.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps reduce the production of sebum, thereby controlling oily skin.
Limonene: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in regulating skin oil production and minimizing the appearance of pores.
Terpinen-4-ol: Possesses antibacterial properties, helping to prevent bacterial growth that can contribute to oily skin and acne.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are widely used to produce tea tree oil, which is known for its antimicrobial properties that help to reduce acne and control oily skin.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia are also used to produce essential oil, which is valued for its astringent properties that help to balance the skin's pH and reduce oil production.
Barks: The barks of the tree are another source of essential oil, which contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections that contribute to oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers, usually in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Steam distill the plant material for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil from the leaves and flowers.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separating funnel, resulting in 10-15ml of oil.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and particulate matter, taking about 30 minutes.
Store the final product in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, away from direct sunlight and heat sources, for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. The oil contains thymol, which has the ability to reduce sebum production, thereby controlling oiliness. Additionally, thymus essential oil has antibacterial properties that help to combat acne and inflammation associated with oily skin. Its invigorating and refreshing scent also helps to balance the skin's natural pH, leaving it looking clearer and more even-toned. This makes it an excellent natural remedy for managing oily skin.
remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: Helps to reduce sebum production and control oiliness by its antimicrobial properties, which prevent the growth of bacteria that can contribute to acne and oily skin.
Thymol: Acts as a natural astringent, reducing the appearance of pores and controlling excess oil secretion, while also possessing antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infections.

Borneol: Functions as a natural antiseptic and astringent, helping to reduce sebum production, tighten pores, and control oiliness, while also possessing anti-inflammatory properties to soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of thymol, a natural antiseptic and astringent that helps control oil production.
Stems: They also contain thymol and other compounds that help reduce sebum production and regulate skin pH.
Buds: The buds are rich in camphor, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can clog pores, making them a useful part for oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers at peak oil content in early morning.
Dry the harvested material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried thymus vulgaris material at 100-110°c to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water and other impurities through decantation.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper or cloth filter to remove any remaining impurities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with oily skin because it contains antimicrobial and antifungal properties that reduce the production of sebum, thereby controlling oiliness. The oil's astringent properties also help tighten pores, reducing their appearance and minimizing oil secretion. Additionally, its antioxidant properties combat free radicals that can lead to inflammation and clogged pores, promoting a balanced skin tone and reducing the visibility of pores. This results in a healthier, more even complexion.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as an antiseptic and astringent, helping to control sebum production and reduce oiliness of the skin.
Bornyl acetate: Exhibits antiseptic and antifungal properties, which can help to minimize inflammation and prevent clogged pores associated with oily skin.
Bornene: Possesses antiseptic and antifungal properties, helping to balance skin's natural pH and reduce sebum production, thus alleviating oily skin symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Rosmarinus officinalis are used to make essential oils because they contain high amounts of camphor, which helps to reduce oil production and balance the skin's pH.

Stems: The stems of Rosmarinus officinalis are used to make essential oils because they contain a higher concentration of 1,8-cineole, which has antiseptic and astringent properties that help to control oiliness.
Flowers: The flowers of Rosmarinus officinalis are used to make essential oils because they have a higher concentration of borneol, which helps to reduce inflammation and prevent clogged pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature leaves from the rosmarinus officinalis plants early in the morning when the dew is still present.
Steam distillation is used to extract the essential oil from the rosmarinus officinalis leaves at a temperature of 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distilled water as it cools down over the next 2 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any remaining impurities and water residue.
Store the extracted rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a cool dark place in glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent properties that balance the skin's oil production, reducing excess sebum. The oil's antiseptic and antifungal properties help control the growth of bacteria that can clog pores, further contributing to oily skin. Additionally, its citrusy scent has a refreshing effect, making it a popular choice in skincare products to help regulate and maintain a healthy, balanced complexion. Regular use can lead to a smoother, clearer appearance.
remedy_constituents: Cineol (Eucalyptol): This terpene constituent helps to reduce sebum production, thereby controlling oily skin by inhibiting the activity of the sebaceous glands.

Limonene: This terpene constituent has astringent and antiseptic properties, helping to reduce excessive sebum production, prevent clogged pores, and prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause acne.
Pinene: This terpene constituent helps to reduce inflammation and regulate sebum production, making it beneficial in controlling oily skin and preventing acne.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part for essential oil production due to their high concentration of citral, a compound that helps balance oil production and reduce acne.
Stems: Stems are another widely used part for essential oils, as they contain a significant amount of citronellal, which helps to regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are also utilized for essential oil production, as they are rich in geraniol and other compounds that help to control oil production, reduce acne, and soothe the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present on the surface.
Steam distill the harvested leaves in a 1:5 ratio with water for 2 hours to release the essential oils.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil layer from the water layer using a separator or decanter.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and sediment particles.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and decongestant properties. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation and unclog pores can help to minimize the appearance of pores and reduce sebum production. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties can help to combat bacteria that can exacerbate oily skin conditions. By balancing skin's natural moisture levels and reducing oil production, Eucalyptus globulus essential oils can promote a healthier and more balanced complexion. Regular use may lead to improved skin texture and reduced acne.
remedy_constituents: 1,8-Cineole: This monoterpene helps to reduce sebum production and has astringent properties, which helps to control oiliness and tighten pores.
Catechin: As a phenolic compound, catechin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and prevent clogged pores that can contribute to oily skin.

Limonene: This terpene has a drying effect on the skin, helping to reduce excess oil and prevent clogged pores, while also having antimicrobial properties that can help to prevent infections and breakouts.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of eucalyptus essential oil, which has a decongestant and astringent property that helps reduce oil production and balance the skin.
Barks: The bark contains a high concentration of eucalyptol, a compound that has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing acne and oily skin.

Stems: The stems of Eucalyptus globulus contain a high amount of eucalyptol and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and control oil production, making them suitable for oily skin essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature eucalyptus globulus leaves in early morning when they are at their highest oil content about 200g.
Steam distill the leaves in a still with a water ratio of 1:3 for 3 hours to release the oil.
Collect the distillate and separate the oil from the water through decantation or centrifugation.
Filter the oil through activated charcoal to remove impurities and improve its clarity and color.
Store the final eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from heat and light.

remedies.remedy_desc: Pelargonium graveolens essential oils helps with oily skin because it has a balancing effect on the skin's natural oil production. The oil's antiseptic and astringent properties help to reduce excess sebum, while its antioxidant properties protect the skin from environmental damage and inflammation. Additionally, the oil's ability to regulate the skin's pH level can help to minimize the appearance of pores, leaving the skin looking smoother and more even-toned. This results in a more balanced and healthier-looking complexion.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: Helps reduce oily skin by promoting the breakdown of sebum, thereby balancing skin moisture levels.
Linalool: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that help to decrease redness and irritation associated with oily skin conditions.
Geraniol: Possesses astringent properties that help to tighten pores, reducing the appearance of oiliness on the skin's surface.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high content of geraniol and linalool, which help balance and regulate sebum production.
Flowers: Flowers are used due to their high concentration of geraniol, which has antiseptic and astringent properties that can help control oil production.
Stems: Stems are used due to their high content of linalool and geraniol, which can help to regulate sebum production and reduce oiliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves and flowers of the plant in the early morning after dew has evaporated to prevent contamination.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at a temperature not exceeding 100°c for 3 to 4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove any impurities immediately.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed tightly with a dropper top cap.
Label the bottle with the name of the plant and the date of distillation for future reference.

remedies.remedy_desc: Geranium maculatum essential oils helps with oily skin because it has natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help balance the skin's pH, reducing the production of excess oil. Its ability to regulate hormonal imbalances also contributes to its efficacy in managing oily skin. Additionally, the oil's antifungal and antibacterial properties help prevent breakouts and infections, promoting a healthier and more balanced skin tone. As a result, the skin appears smoother and more even-toned.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps to reduce sebum production and balance the skin's natural oil levels, thus controlling oily skin.
β-Caryophyllene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and calm oily skin, reducing the risk of acne and other skin irritations.
Geraniol: Acts as an astringent and antiseptic agent, helping to tighten the skin's pores and reduce oil secretion, thereby controlling oily skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, which help to control sebum production and reduce the appearance of pores.
Roots: Contain compounds that help to balance skin pH and reduce inflammation, making it suitable for oily skin.
Flowers: High in geraniol, which has antiseptic and astringent properties that help to control excess oil and reduce acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature plants with dark green leaves and clusters of pink flowers in the early morning.
Steam distillation requires 20-30% plant material and a distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a glass container with a tight-fitting lid immediately.
Store the essential oil in a cool dark place with airtight containers to preserve its quality.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cotton gauze before bottling for purity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon nardus essential oils helps with oily skin because it has natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation. The oil's ability to balance the skin's pH levels and tighten pores also contributes to its effectiveness in managing oily skin. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Cymbopogon nardus essential oil help control the growth of bacteria that can clog pores and exacerbate oily skin issues. This makes it a popular natural remedy for those struggling with oily skin.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene helps reduce sebum production and inflammation associated with oily skin.
Limonene: This monoterpene has astringent and antiseptic properties that help control oil production and prevent clogged pores.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene exhibits anti-inflammatory and antiseborrheic properties, which help to reduce redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make essential oils due to their high content of citronella and limonene, which have natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help balance oily skin.
Stems: They are used to make essential oils because they contain a high concentration of citronellal, a natural decongestant that helps to reduce oil production and control acne.

Rhyzomes (also known as Rhizomes): They are used to make essential oils as they contain a high concentration of geraniol and linalool, which have antiseptic and antifungal properties that help to balance oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh leaves of cymbopogon nardus at dawn to minimize oil content degradation.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to release the oil.
Separate the distilled oil from water using a condenser and collect the oil in a container.
Filter the collected oil through activated carbon to remove impurities and improve its clarity.
Store the filtered oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight seal at room temperature for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antimicrobial properties. It helps to regulate oil production in the skin, reducing the appearance of pores and minimizing oiliness. The oil's antiseptic properties also help to combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to oily skin. Additionally, Salvia officinalis essential oils has a balancing effect on the skin's natural pH, helping to maintain a healthy skin balance and reducing the risk of breakouts and other skin issues.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: Helps with oily skin by reducing sebum production and balancing the skin's natural oil levels.
Caryophyllene oxide: Contributes to reducing inflammation and excess oil production in the skin, leading to a more balanced skin tone.
Ursolic acid: Helps to regulate the skin's natural barrier function, reducing oiliness and preventing clogged pores.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for essential oil production due to their high concentration of salvene and camphor, which help to balance oil production in the skin.
Stems: The stems of Salvia officinalis are also used for essential oil production, as they contain a significant amount of salvene and borneol, which help to reduce inflammation and purify the skin.
Flowers: The flowers are used to create a floral and slightly sweet essential oil that can help to regulate oil production and reduce the appearance of pores, making it beneficial for oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the early morning to ensure highest oil content and quality.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves thoroughly with a clean towel to prevent contamination.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a 20% solution with distilled water at a temperature of 100 degrees celsius for 3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation water using a separatory funnel and collect the oil.
Store the extracted salvia officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at a cool temperature.
